the_signal.headline: Agent-to-Agent protocols move from theory to production necessity
the_signal.body: n8n's deep dive on A2A protocol specification, failure modes, and enterprise orchestration signals that cross-agent communication is now table-stakes for scaling multi-agent systems. GTM teams deploying revenue automation with multiple AI agents need to architect for interop now—not patch it later when agents start siloing data and decisions.

tool_drop.name: Locofy
tool_drop.what: Design-to-code agent that converts designs directly into production code
tool_drop.why: Reduces engineering bottleneck in GTM iteration cycles—ship product variations and landing page tests without waiting for dev sprints

thread_of_day.title: Production AI Playbook: Complex Agent Patterns
thread_of_day.takeaway: Your first agent scales; three agents become undebuggable without proper multi-agent architecture, memory isolation, and sub-workflow composition patterns built in from day one

quote.text: Your first AI agent worked great. Then you added three more and the system became impossible to debug.

whats_moving.context: New vision capability from DeepSeek signals competitive pressure on Claude/GPT-4V pricing and latency—matters for GTM engineers choosing vision models for document processing in deal workflows
Enterprise AI orchestration now requires formal A2A specs and failure handling; this is infrastructure GTM automation systems can't ignore at scale
Enterprise compliance for AI systems is becoming standardized—GTM teams deploying autonomous revenue systems need governance selection criteria for AML, PII, and audit trails

act_on_this: Map your multi-agent architecture against n8n's A2A protocol spec—identify where agents communicate and add explicit handoff contracts, retry logic, and observability hooks before adding the fourth agent to your revenue stack.

numbers.stat: n8n targeting 200-person UK team by 2029 (tripled existing UK team in 2025 alone)
numbers.context: Workflow automation platform expansion accelerating into enterprise—signals demand for orchestration tooling is real and consolidating around platforms that handle complexity at scale
